reasoning:
The baseline rate for 'Strategic developments' in Comoros is ~0.5 events per 30-day period. Assuming a Poisson distribution, the probability of at least one event in any given 30-day window is roughly 39%. 

For the first resolution date (Feb 22, 2026), the 30-day window covers Jan 23 to Feb 22. We are already 23 days into this window. English news searches revealed no major strategic developments, but local French sources (YouTube videos from Jan 25-26, 2026) mention foreign military presence ("militaires étrangers," "présence militaire américaine") and potential controversies. These could correspond to a military agreement, visit, or deployment, which ACLED frequently codes as 'Strategic developments'. Even so, it's not a verified high-profile event. Factoring in the remaining 7 days of the window and the uncertainty around whether ACLED will code these rumors, I estimate the probability at 32% (lower than base rate because a major event would likely have been more visible).

For the second resolution date (Mar 17, 2026), the 30-day window begins exactly on the forecast date (Feb 15) and runs purely into the future. I rely on the base rate (~39%), adjusted slightly upwards (42%) because the recent local political/military rumors suggest a slightly heightened tense or active environment. This also properly reflects increasing uncertainty toward 0.5 at a longer horizon.
